meta.product: Frontier Watch — AI x Supply Chain
meta.reader_persona: Supply chain professional; fluent in SC/business language; novice on tech-stack jargon. Use for role-play verification.

editorial_conventions.impact: 1-5 editorial: consequence for a mid-market supply chain org if the signal holds
editorial_conventions.adopt: 1-5 editorial: how directly the org stack can adopt/mimic today
editorial_conventions.multi_source: substance independently reported/evidenced by >=2 unaffiliated organizations; publisher families (e.g., BizClik titles, Industry Dive titles) count as one voice
editorial_conventions.vendor_figures: always labeled as claims, never independently verified

meta.taxonomy: v2 (2026-07-03): plan/source/make/deliver/sell + infra (cross-cutting); SCOR-based; legacy keys mapped; cannabis lens standing sweep with cann:true flag
meta.signal_target: 12-18 per edition (widened Jul 3 for taxonomy v2's six categories)

patterns.title: From insight to execution: AI that acts, not advises
patterns.verdict_1line: E03: the customer side arrived — FedEx targets agents in >50% of workflows by 2028, Walmart runs agentic end-to-end flows; all three planning majors ship in-model agents. Targets and architecture, not yet audited results.
confirmers: autonomy percentages appear in customer (not vendor) case studies — FedEx 2028 target and Walmart flows are plans/architecture, not audited results
SAP Outbound agent GA confirmation (now two editions past Q2 target)
Oracle agentic apps earn named-customer autonomy numbers

patterns.title: The moat is business context, not the model
patterns.verdict_1line: E03: quiet by the counting rule (no new priced voice), but architecturally corroborated — Kinaxis/o9/Blue Yonder converging on agents-inside-the-model is the context thesis restated by the planning market itself.
confirmers: 'context layer' appears as its own priced SKU/line item (watch AVEVA+Cognite packaging post-close)
weakeners: frontier models get good enough at raw-table reasoning that definition layers stop mattering (no evidence yet)

patterns.title: AI economics shift from seats to outcomes and tokens
patterns.verdict_1line: E03: holding, not advancing — no new org joined; GPT-5.6's three price tiers keep token economics moving under agent contracts. Next tell unchanged: Acumatica AI Units terms at 2026 R2 GA.
confirmers: outcome terms appear in mid-market ERP quotes (Acumatica AI Units at 2026 R2 GA is the tell)
weakeners: outcome pricing quietly reverts to consumption floors and minimums

patterns.title: Data readiness is the binding constraint
patterns.verdict_1line: E03: consensus stands, no new counting additions; inverse-case echo from cannabis (S13) — where regulation mandates the data foundation, the AI layer forms fast on top.

patterns.title: The plumbing is standardizing: MCP + A2A move from experiment to default
patterns.verdict_1line: E03: both ends fired — RC formally published (stateless core, Tasks/Apps extensions, deprecation policy; implementers corroborate) AND the E01-named confirmer landed: NetSuite ships a first-party MCP connector in an operational mid-market ERP.
confirmers: 2026-07-28 spec ships as specced — CHECK IN E05 (Jul 30 edition), after the final lands; E04 (Jul 23) is the last pre-final read
Acumatica answers with first-party MCP at 2026 R2 (NetSuite set the reference posture)
more operational-stack vendors (WMS, TMS) ship first-party MCP connectors
weakeners: value migrating to proprietary layers on top of the open core

patterns.title: Foundation forecasters are the new baseline — routing and hybrids win
patterns.verdict_1line: E03: SECOND consecutive quiet cycle — no new independent forecaster tests despite full plan sweeps; faint echo in model-tier routing at the LLM layer (GPT-5.6 tiers). If E04 is also silent, run a deliberate deep-dive (targeted arXiv/benchmark sweep) instead of passive sweeps.
confirmers: planning vendors expose model-routing controls instead of one embedded model (S03's converged suites are where to look)
weakeners: a next-gen pre-trained model decisively closes the stable-series gap in independent tests

watch_items.item: MCP 2026-07-28 spec final lands — confirm shipped as specced (stateless core, Tasks/Apps extensions, deprecation policy). E03: RC formally published; implementers corroborate. NOTE: confirmable only in E05 (Jul 30), after the final lands.

SAP Outbound Task Orchestration Agent GA — now TWO editions past the Q2 2026 target with no GA confirmation (E02, E03). Carried as unconfirmed/possible slip; treat a third silent edition as a soft weakener for vendor GA claims.
Fabric planning GA rollout completion; overlap check vs in-house capacity/WIP planning tools. E03: nothing observed — third silent cycle.

GPU-accelerated Fabric Warehouse early access — RESOLVED: window independently confirmed open (Microsoft Fabric blog + independent coverage; UNC Health 5x early adopter). M7 benchmark itself still pending user action.

Acumatica 2026 R2 launch: AI Assistant GA terms + AI Units pricing (outcome/consumption structure) — read floors and outcome definitions (M9). E03 adds: does R2 also answer NetSuite with first-party MCP (M13)?
AVEVA + Cognite packaging post-close: does 'context' become its own priced line item (P2 confirmer)? E03: nothing new observed.
Q3 earnings-call language check: do vendors quietly re-describe 'agents' as 'assistants' (P1 weakener)? Opened E03 now that customer-side commitments (FedEx, Walmart) raise the stakes.
P6 quiet-cycle guard: if E04's plan sweep again surfaces no independent forecaster tests, run a deliberate deep-dive (targeted arXiv / benchmark search) — chronic zero-states are a research failure.

open_actions.action: Benchmark Chronos-2 / TimesFM 2.5 zero-shot vs existing WAPE+bias framework on a known SKU's POS history
Read-only Fabric data agent over governed Lakehouse tables (MRP/WIP) + one scheduled unattended run
open_actions.status: unknown (unattended runs E02-E03 — carried forward). E03 note: data agents now GA with AI-guided setup — pilot cost dropped.

open_actions.action: Pilot Agent Skills for Power BI + Power BI Modeling MCP server on a non-production semantic model
open_actions.status: unknown (unattended runs E02-E03 — carried forward). Gated by M11 (post-Jul 28 spec validation).

open_actions.action: AI pass over Onematica BOMs/routings flagging structural anomalies pre-cutover
Verify Onematica upgrade path/timeline to Acumatica 2026 R1 and AI-feature licensing
open_actions.note: gates build-vs-wait on several backlog items — ask user first

open_actions.action: Adopt SADA loop + human gate as written template for internal agent pilots
Request GPU-accelerated Fabric Warehouse early-access preview on a non-production workspace; benchmark heaviest month-end workload before/after — score wall-clock AND capacity-unit consumption
open_actions.status: proposed — E03: access window confirmed open, ready to execute

open_actions.action: Prototype agent-assisted order intake: parse emailed POs against Onematica items/customers via Power Automate + AI Builder; human confirms draft sales orders
Add outcome-pricing questions (billable outcome definition, floors/minimums, audit rights) to every AI purchase/renewal
open_actions.status: proposed (carried E02→E03; applies to Acumatica AI Units at R2)

open_actions.action: Extend SADA-plus-gates pilot template with Basware-style authority tiers (unassisted / review / always-escalate) per AP flow
Hold new MCP connector work until Jul 28 final; then validate Power BI Modeling MCP server against final spec before M3 pilot
open_actions.status: proposed (carried E02→E03; 12 days to final at E03 compile)

open_actions.action: Track agent-workspace form factor (Oracle E02; echoed by Kinaxis/o9/Blue Yonder E03) as the pattern to expect from Acumatica at 2026 R2
Ask Acumatica the first-party MCP question at every 2026 R2 touchpoint; meanwhile pilot CData's Acumatica MCP server read-only on a non-production tenant (post-Jul 28)

Digital-twin-lite: what-if replica of one flow (DC outbound day or one plant's WIP) in a Fabric notebook on Lakehouse data; test one policy change against replayed history; deliverable = decision memo

signal_registry.title: Gartner 2026 SC tech trends: agentic + physical AI, decision governance
Pre-trained forecasters mature into baselines; routing beats universal deployment
AWS Amazon Connect Decisions: planning tools as AI teammates on SCOT models
Blue Yonder ICON: agents as execution layer; small in-house models for cost
Planning platforms push into procurement (single-publisher caveat: BizClik)
Supplier master data built for AP can't support AI (tariff era)
C.H. Robinson claims closed-loop AI runs 92% of 4PL shipments (vendor claim)
Production agents ship: SAP routings-from-BOM agent; Schneider+MSFT 50% claim
Adoption barometer: 6%->24% expectation vs MIT 5% scale rate
Salesforce: Fin $3.6B + Help Agent priced per resolution
Build 2026: Fabric IQ GA on Power BI semantic models; context-layer thesis
Agent Skills for Power BI + Power BI Modeling MCP server (authoring via governed layer)
Fabric June update: service principals for data agents; per-call cost stats
MCP 2026-07-28 spec RC: stateless core, Tasks, MCP Apps; adoption stats
Acumatica 2026 R1: AI Studio, assistant (early access), anomaly reports
Autonomous fulfillment-as-a-service: Locus Array; Exol six-site FaaS network (rescan Jul 3)
Cannabis AI proof points: ~20% yield claim (Neatleaf/PURPLEFARM), Flora $401M Zero Gravity (BCC report; rescan Jul 3)
Salesforce Agentforce Commerce GA: Shopper/Buyer/Merchant agents inside ChatGPT + Google
commercetools + Mirion B2B Intake Agent: unstructured orders to ready-to-action quotes
Oracle ships four agentic applications for Fusion SCM (single-voice PR)
Adoption gap quantified: 85% raising spend vs 10% live (Sage / First Analysis)
Basware Governed Autonomy Framework for finance AI
Procurement AI to scaled deployment: long-tail POs, autonomous sourcing (trend synthesis)
Cannabis AI bifurcates: compliance automation + cultivation intelligence (trend synthesis)
Schneider Electric to acquire Cognite — buying the industrial context layer
Samsung AI-driven factories by 2030 (ctx; governance-paired autonomy roadmap)
FarEye PILOT: 11-agent MCP-first dispatcher (vendor claims labeled)
Logistics consolidation week: CMA CGM/FedEx SC $1.4B; Descartes, Symbotic AI tuck-ins
MCP 2026-07-28 final holds; implementers validate stateless core + Tasks (countdown card)
GPU-accelerated Fabric Warehouse July early-access window (CoddSpeed; SIGMOD paper)
Outcome pricing operational: Deloitte accounting guidance; SAP Joule outcome/consumption billing
ARC white papers: the decision intelligence layer (context, governance, thresholds, pathways)
FedEx commits the network: agents targeted into >50% of operational workflows by 2028; FedEx Freight rebuilds LTL stack AI-first
Walmart scales agentic workflows + digital twins; four 'super agents' (Industry Dive single family — flagged)
Planning majors converge: Kinaxis/o9/Blue Yonder all ship in-model agents (trend synthesis)
Autonomous negotiation on tail spend posts claimed savings (~4.7%); procurement agents scale (trend synthesis)
Factory-manager agent stack commercializes: NVIDIA blueprint, Invisible AI vision agents, IMTS preview (trend synthesis)
AI as freight demand: data-center cargo lifts Asia->NA air volumes ~20%, June spot rates ~36% (single brief; candidate new pattern: AI as demand-side force)
Prime Day 2026 as agentic-commerce stress test: $26.4B; AI-referred visitors ~40% likelier to buy; Alexa for Shopping
Salesforce Agentic Order Management: plain-language fulfillment rules across 7k+ locations (follow-up to E02 S01)
Fabric data agents GA + guided setup; GPU Warehouse early access confirmed open (UNC Health 5x)
NetSuite first-party MCP connector (AI Connector Service + Mar Companion wave) — P5 confirmer fired; Acumatica third-party only (CData)
GPT-5.6 ships in three price tiers after CAISI pre-release review — model layer as managed supply input
MCP 2026-07-28 RC formally published: stateless core, Tasks/Apps extensions, deprecation policy (12 days to final)
Cannabis: compliance rails consolidate (NY BioTrack->Metrc; hemp onboarding) — AI layer forming on top (single specialist source for AI substance)

lessons: Role-play persona must state reader tech fluency explicitly; defaults drift to the builder's level (E01 draft-1 failure).
Glossary (TERMS) and Stack-detail line are template-level; append terms, never strip the layer.
Check publisher families before setting multi:true.
Timeline right-hand markers are third-party forecasts, label as such.

Template Rev E (Jul 3): dual REGISTERS — write exec copy (xone/xwhy) separately from analyst copy for every signal; palette bound to view (warm exec / cool analyst, green brand accent); DELTA object + pattern move chips power the weekly pulse strip; append weekly item to feed.xml; update data-ed numeral; PWA assets (icons/manifest/sw) are permanent — never remove.

Rev F editorial rules (Jul 3): never embed UI pills in running prose; badges nowrap; 62ch reading measures; one green data accent; app icon = approved radar-sweep mark (no redesign/recolor without sign-off); 390px mobile checklist incl. notch clearance runs every edition.

Taxonomy v2 + cannabis lens (Jul 3, Rev G): six locked keys (plan/source/make/deliver/sell/infra); value-chain ribbon + infrastructure foundation band replace filter chips; every research week sweeps all 5 stages + infra + cannabis industry; cann:true signals feed the conditional Industry Lens section (auto-hidden when none qualify); stage-focus line must show honest thin/empty states.

E02 (Jul 9, unattended): mimic-board 'from' links must reference current-edition signal codes — board is edition-scoped (M7-M12), while open_actions in state carries all editions; role-play subagent caught exec-register mechanics leakage in shared 'what' blocks (S12/S13) — shared blocks must pass the exec bar too, since both registers render them.

E03 (Jul 16, unattended): role-play subagents caught (a) date-logic errors in forward-looking copy — check every 'next edition confirms X' claim against the actual calendar (a Jul 23 edition cannot confirm a Jul 28 ship); (b) orphan numbers referencing prior editions ($262B) — attribute cross-edition figures explicitly; (c) internal shorthand (Onematica, SADA) needs TERMS glosses since every edition has potential first-time readers; (d) the scatter caption had a hardcoded signal range (S01-S15) — now genericized, keep it generic; (e) assertions.js still hardcodes 'search stateless finds S12' — keep the MCP-spec card at code S12 (or generalize the assertion when next editing the harness is authorized).
